title: layia nemorosa
intro: Layia nemorosa, also known as the mountain dandelion or white tidy tips, is a flowering plant native to western North America. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly the root and leaves, which contain compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Layia nemorosa have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Layia nemorosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
